facts.statement: A black hole is a region of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape.
questions: Can light escape from a black hole?
What happens when gravity becomes extremely strong in spacetime?

facts.statement: The event horizon is the boundary beyond which escape from a black hole is impossible.
questions: What is the boundary of a black hole called?
Beyond what point can nothing escape a black hole?

facts.statement: Black holes form when massive stars collapse at the end of their lives.
questions: What happens when massive stars collapse?
What astronomical event creates a black hole?

facts.statement: The singularity is the point of infinite density at the center of a black hole.
questions: What is the singularity in a black hole?
What is at the center of a black hole?
What part of a black hole has infinite density?

facts.statement: In 2019 the Event Horizon Telescope produced the first image of a black hole.
questions: When was the first image of a black hole taken?
What telescope produced the first black hole image?
What did the Event Horizon Telescope accomplish in 2019?

facts.statement: The first black hole image was of a black hole in the galaxy M87.
questions: Which black hole was photographed first?
In which galaxy is the first imaged black hole located?
What galaxy contains the first black hole ever photographed?
